titles.title: CGView.js: a JavaScript package for visualizing small genomes

citation.article_title: Circular genome visualization and exploration using CGView.
citation.author: Stothard
citation.unstructured_citation: Stothard, P., & Wishart, D. S. (2005). Circular genome visualization and exploration using CGView. Bioinformatics (Oxford, England), 21(4), 537–539. https://doi.org/10.1093/bioinformatics/bti054

citation.article_title: Proksee: in-depth characterization and visualization of bacterial genomes
citation.author: Grant
citation.unstructured_citation: Grant, J. R., Enns, E., Marinier, E., Mandal, A., Herman, E. K., Chen, C., Graham, M., Domselaar, G. V., & Stothard, P. (2023). Proksee: in-depth characterization and visualization of bacterial genomes. Nucleic Acids Research, 51(W1), W484–W492. https://doi.org/10.1093/nar/gkad326

citation.article_title: PlasMapper 3.0—a web server for generating, editing, annotating and visualizing publication quality plasmid maps

PlasMapper 3.0—a web server for generating, editing, annotating and visualizing publication quality plasmid maps. Nucleic Acids Research. https://doi.org/10.1093/nar/gkad276

citation.article_title: BASys2: a next-generation bacterial genome annotation system

MOBHunter: a data integration platform for identification and classification of mobile genetic elements in microbial genomes. Nucleic Acids Research, gkaf396. https://doi.org/10.1093/nar/gkaf396

citation.article_title: The PLSDB 2025 update: enhanced annotations and improved functionality for comprehensive plasmid research
citation.author: Molano

citation.article_title: Circos: an information aesthetic for comparative genomics.
citation.author: Krzywinski
citation.unstructured_citation: Krzywinski, M., Schein, J., Birol, I., Connors, J., Gascoyne, R., Horsman, D., Jones, S. J., & Marra, M. A. (2009). Circos: an information aesthetic for comparative genomics. Genome Research, 19(9), 1639–1645. https://doi.org/10.1101/gr.092759.109

citation.article_title: JBrowse 2: a modular genome browser with views of synteny and structural variation

JBrowse 2: a modular genome browser with views of synteny and structural variation. Genome Biology, 24(1), 74. https://doi.org/10.1186/s13059-023-02914-z

citation.article_title: pileup.js: a JavaScript library for interactive and in-browser visualization of genomic data
citation.author: Vanderkam
citation.unstructured_citation: Vanderkam, D., Aksoy, B. A., Hodes, I., Perrone, J., & Hammerbacher, J. (2016). pileup.js: a JavaScript library for interactive and in-browser visualization of genomic data. Bioinformatics, 32(15), 2378–2379. https://doi.org/10.1093/bioinformatics/btw167

citation.article_title: igv.js: an embeddable JavaScript implementation of the Integrative Genomics Viewer (IGV)
citation.author: Robinson
citation.unstructured_citation: Robinson, J. T., Thorvaldsdottir, H., Turner, D., & Mesirov, J. P. (2022). igv.js: an embeddable JavaScript implementation of the Integrative Genomics Viewer (IGV). Bioinformatics, 39(1), btac830. https://doi.org/10.1093/bioinformatics/btac830

citation.article_title: HLRMDB: a comprehensive database of the human microbiome with metagenomic assembly, taxonomic classification, and functional annotation by analysis of long-read and hybrid sequencing data
citation.author: Zhai
citation.unstructured_citation: Zhai, Z., Che, X., Shen, W., Zhang, Z., Li, Y., & Pan, J. (2025). HLRMDB: a comprehensive database of the human microbiome with metagenomic assembly, taxonomic classification, and functional annotation by analysis of long-read and hybrid sequencing data. Nucleic Acids Research, gkaf1152. https://doi.org/10.1093/nar/gkaf1152
